title: Feature Branch Workflow
description: Create a feature branch, make commits on it, switch back to main, and merge the feature in.
starterCode:
# Write shell commands to:
# 1. Create and switch to a branch called feature/add-login
#    (use git switch -c or git checkout -b)
# 2. Make two commits on the feature branch
#    (you can echo files and commit them)
# 3. Switch back to the main branch
# 4. Merge the feature/add-login branch into main
# 5. Delete the feature/add-login branch
solutionCode:
git switch -c feature/add-login
echo "<form>Login</form>" > login.html
git add login.html
git commit -m "Add login form"
echo "function validate() {}" > validate.js
git add validate.js
git commit -m "Add form validation"
git switch main
git merge feature/add-login
git branch -d feature/add-login
hints: git switch -c <branch-name> creates and switches to the branch in one step
Make two separate commits — each with git add then git commit -m
git switch main (or git checkout main) returns you to the main branch
git merge feature/add-login merges the feature into your current branch
git branch -d deletes a branch safely (only if it is already merged)

validationRules.errorMessage: Create and switch to feature/add-login using git switch -c or git checkout -b
Make at least one commit on the feature branch
Switch back to main branch using git switch main or git checkout main
Merge the feature branch into main with git merge feature/add-login
Delete the feature branch after merging with git branch -d feature/add-login
